questions.question: What is a Queue data structure?
options: A) A collection of elements that can be accessed in First-In-First-Out (FIFO) order
B) A collection of elements that can be accessed in Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) order
C) A collection of elements that can be accessed in Random Order
D) A collection of elements that can be accessed in Sorted Order
questions.explanation: A Queue is a linear data structure that follows the FIFO principle, where the first element that is added to the queue will be the first one to be removed.

questions.question: Which operation is performed on the front of the queue?
questions.explanation: The Enqueue operation is performed on the back of the queue, while the Dequeue operation is performed on the front of the queue.

questions.question: What is an advantage of implementing a queue using an array?
questions.explanation: Implementing a queue using an array is more memory efficient because it only requires a fixed amount of memory to store the elements.

questions.question: Which type of queue allows elements to be added and removed from both ends?
questions.explanation: A Double-Ended Queue is a type of queue that allows elements to be added and removed from both ends.

questions.question: What is the primary function of a priority queue?
options: A) To store elements in a specific order
B) To implement the FIFO principle
C) To prioritize elements based on their priority
questions.explanation: A Priority Queue is a type of queue that prioritizes elements based on their priority, allowing the highest-priority element to be removed first.
